key_insight: This survey frames graph neural networks as the preferred model class for relational data, while showing that their value depends on when graph structure justifies its computational cost and where it fails to improve separation power.
engineering_relevance: For materials discovery, the paper highlights how graph construction, architecture choice, and evaluation rigor determine whether GNNs produce useful gains or just benchmark artefacts.
method: Unifies spectral and spatial GNN formulations under a shared design space.
Connects model expressiveness to the Weisfeiler-Leman hierarchy.
Compares 12 application domains to identify recurring strengths, failure modes, and deployment gaps.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a cross-domain survey that contrasts reported gains with baseline quality and split effects, rather than relying on a single benchmark or dataset.
watch_item: Performance can be overstated when graph construction is expensive, baselines are weak, or the evaluation split is overly favorable; temporal, heterophilous, and large-scale settings remain especially difficult.
paper_title: Graph Neural Networks Applications Across Domains: All Insights You Need
